work_unit: SZ-BG-H-002: blobToBase64 O(n²) string concat → chunked O(n)
situation: audit-2026-07-07-047 finding SZ-BG-H-002 (high): blobToBase64 uses str += String.fromCharCode(bytes[i]) in a loop for base64 encoding. For a 4MB image blob this causes ~4 million string reallocations (O(n²)), blocking the service worker and potentially causing OOM on large OCR inputs.
action: Replaced O(n²) string concatenation with chunked String.fromCharCode.apply (8192-byte chunks) + array join, yielding O(n) performance. Used case-104 escape hatch (direct wt edit + wt-internal commit) because opt-background-1783213292 had diverged from main (5 stacked commits from prior SZ-BG fixes).
outcome_evidence: Commit 9b35dd4 on auto/opt-background-1783213292 pushed to origin. Function body changed from 4-line loop concat to 5-line chunked apply+joint. No other call sites modified (blobToBase64 called at lines 189/219/519/531/629 — all benefit automatically).
next_suggestion: SZ-SH-H-001: host_permissions grants <all_urls> but extension never uses tab/scripting APIs — remove excess permissions (manifest.json, high severity)

audit_findings.finding: blobToBase64 O(n²) string concatenation via str += charCode in loop
audit_findings.remediation: Replace with chunked String.fromCharCode.apply + array join
